method: real qwen_real_forward residual stream over the 6-prompt campaign holdout; router logits softmaxed over all 128 experts then top-8 (norm_topk_prob); lm_head and final norm skipped (routing does not read them)

sampling_error.binomial_note: count_e ~ Binomial(n_tokens, p_e), variance floored at the null p_med(1-p_med) so never-routed experts are not scored as easy. The 8 picks per token are drawn without replacement and are correlated across experts, so treat this as an order-of-magnitude floor, not a tight requirement.

trust_verdict: NOT trustworthy for PER-EXPERT bit allocation. Only 81.0 percent of (layer, expert) hot/cold assignments survive token resampling at 95 percent, and 2.1 percent of experts are never routed at all on 1313 tokens (expected count per expert is only 82.062). A stable median partition needs roughly 7448 tokens (median expert: 290). Until then use the routing signal only as a COARSE aggregate (quartile bands, or the lower-variance softmax_mass which has no zeros), and gate any allocation on a real parent-vs-packed forward.
partition_definition: hot = top half of the 128 experts by top8_count within the layer (ties broken by softmax_mass); cold = the other half. Exactly 64 hot and 64 cold per layer.
